Class ZerosToEndShiftingTwoDimArray

java.lang.Object
com.prutzkow.twodimarray.TwoDimArray
ru.rsreu.afonin0409.ZerosToEndShiftingTwoDimArray
public class ZerosToEndShiftingTwoDimArray extends com.prutzkow.twodimarray.TwoDimArray
Extension of TwoDimArray class that provides functionality for shifting all zero elements to the end of each row in a two dimensional array. The class also support filling the array with random values from a specified interval.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class com.prutzkow.twodimarray.TwoDimArray

    arrayBody, FORMAT

    Method
    Description

    addArrayToStringBuilderWithFormat(StringBuilder builder, int[] integers)
    Adds the string representation of a one dimensional array to a StringBuilder using formatting for element alignment.
    private void
    fillIntegerListByZeroStartsFromIndex(int startIndex, int[] integers)
    Fills the specified integer array with zeros starting from the given index.

    fillRandomValuesFromInterval(int lowerBound, int upperBound)
    Fills all elements of the two dimensional array with random integers from the interval [lowerBound, upperBound].

    Fills one row of the two dimensional array with random integers from the specified interval.

    Generates a random integer within the specifies interval.

    Moves all zero elements to the end of each row in two dimensional array.

    Moves all zero elements to the end of a one dimensional integer array, preserving the relative order of non-zero elements.

  • Constructor Details

    • ZerosToEndShiftingTwoDimArray

      public ZerosToEndShiftingTwoDimArray(int... sizes) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Parameters:
      sizes - variable number of arguments defining the size of each dimension
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided sizes are invalid

  • Method Details

    • moveZerosToEndForListsInTwoDimensionArray

      public void moveZerosToEndForListsInTwoDimensionArray()
      Moves all zero elements to the end of each row in two dimensional array. For each row of array, the method moveZerosToEndInIntegerList() is called.

    • fillRandomValuesFromInterval

      public void fillRandomValuesFromInterval(int lowerBound, int upperBound)
      Fills all elements of the two dimensional array with random integers from the interval [lowerBound, upperBound].
      Parameters:
      lowerBound - the lowest bound of the interval
      upperBound - the upper bound of the interval

    • moveZerosToEndInIntegerList

      private void moveZerosToEndInIntegerList(int[] integers)
      Moves all zero elements to the end of a one dimensional integer array, preserving the relative order of non-zero elements.
      Parameters:
      integers - the integer array to shifting

    • fillIntegerListByZeroStartsFromIndex

      private void fillIntegerListByZeroStartsFromIndex(int startIndex, int[] integers)
      Fills the specified integer array with zeros starting from the given index.
      Parameters:
      startIndex - the index from which to start filling with zeros
      integers - the integer array to fill

    • fillRowRandomValuesFromInterval

      private void fillRowRandomValuesFromInterval(int[] row, int lowerBound, int upperBound)
      Fills one row of the two dimensional array with random integers from the specified interval.
      Parameters:
      row - the array row to fill
      lowerBound - the lower bound of the interval
      upperBound - the upper bound of the interval

    • getRandomIntegerFromIntercal

      private int getRandomIntegerFromIntercal(int lowerBound, int upperBound)
      Generates a random integer within the specifies interval.
      Parameters:
      lowerBound - the lower bound of the interval
      upperBound - the upper bound of the interval
      Returns:
      a random integer in the range [lowerBound, upperBound]

    • addArrayToStringBuilderWithFormat

      private void addArrayToStringBuilderWithFormat(StringBuilder builder, int[] integers)
      Adds the string representation of a one dimensional array to a StringBuilder using formatting for element alignment.
      Parameters:
      builder - the StringBuilder to which the formatted string is added
      integers - the integer array to format and add

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the two dimensional array. The string contains the count of elements in the first column and all rows of the array with formatted output of elements.
      Overrides:
      toString in class com.prutzkow.twodimarray.TwoDimArray
      Returns:
      a string representation of the two dimensional array
